title: Robust British Celery Yorkshire Pudding
description: A garlicky, light Yorkshire pudding with a unique celery salt kick, made with a long-fermented dough and egg-free-friendly aquafaba for a wholesome British twist.
instructions: In a bowl, combine 200 g all-purpose flour with 1 tsp dry active yeast and a pinch of salt; stir to distribute.
Add 250 ml water to the flour mixture and whisk until smooth; cover and let rest at room temperature for 12 hours to ferment.
While the dough ferments, preheat the oven to 220°C and pour 50 ml olive oil into each cup of a muffin tin, then place the tin in the oven to heat.
After fermentation, whisk 60 ml aquafaba, 2 tsp celery salt, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp fine sea salt, and 0.5 tsp black pepper into the dough until well combined.
Remove the hot muffin tin from the oven and carefully pour the batter into each cup, filling about ¾ full.
Bake for 18 minutes, or until the puddings have risen and turned golden brown.
Let the pudding rest for 2 minutes before serving.
Serve immediately with a drizzle of olive oil and a sprinkle of extra celery salt if desired.
